author: PC Gamer
text:
Time to get a little sadder about Destiny 2: TWIDs are coming to an end
No great surprise, given the end of Destiny 2 update, but seeing seeing the words put to paper is a bummer.

author: PC Gamer
text:
You can just bypass Fable's 'complex, nuanced' reputation system with enough gold
Fable's take on morality sounds pretty neat—if only it weren't so eager to assure us we can just buy out of it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
A single crab has the third highest player kill count in Path of Exile 2
Grinding Gear Games briefly created one of the most powerful monsters in the action RPG—on accident.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Quake Champions gets a huge update and free battle pass to celebrate the 30th anniversary of Quake
No, it is not dead, and if you've ever wanted to jump in, this would be a great time.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Pokémon Go data was used to help train AI systems being developed for military drones
Niantic Spatial says "ground scans" collected through Pokémon Go are part of the data being used to train its models.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Phantom Blade: Zero devs couldn't care less about releasing near GTA 6: 'We don't even think of the competition, what matters is how polished the game is'
They did care about making the game run as well as possible on lower-end hardware, however.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I was big into Clutch's mix of story and racing styles, but the driving's a lot simmier than I expected
I wish there was a little bit less Forza Horizons and a little bit more Burnout in this new narraracer.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Dragon's Dogma 2 hands out an infinite fast travel item after people wouldn't stop moaning about how laborious it was to go anywhere
That's the problem with this generation: iPhones and Eternal Ferrystones.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Phanteks shows off PC case that bucks the fish tank trend in favour of greater thermal efficiency and giving your GPU its own little room
I don't need a new case, I don't need a new case, I don't need a new case…

author: PC Gamer
text:
An SSD company has just agreed to an almost $2 billion multi-year NAND deal, which is a sign it expects the memory crisis to stick around for a while
It could be a very good deal for Biwin if NAND prices continue to rise.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Xbox's new chief strategy officer is still a big believer in in-game ads
Matthew Ball predicted a rise in ad-supported gaming before joining Xbox, and he still thinks it's a good idea.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to take a photo of a Track Toy at Irokawa Space Centre in Forza Horizon 6
Snap a pic of a Track Toy for the Astro Toy photo challenge.

author: PC Gamer
text:
SK hynix claims it will be able to triple its memory chip output by 2034, roughly 10 years sooner than first projected
Just in time to solve the current crisis, right?

author: PC Gamer
text:
EVE Online's survival spinoff Frontier is a hardcore space sim you can play on a gamepad that's unlike anything else out there
Gamepad, Joystick, and WASD is a game changer.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Where to find the Tokyo City region Treasure Hunt in Forza Horizon 6
Climb the warehouses to beat another Festival Playlist Treasure Hunt challenge.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Witchspire is a charming Palworld-esque survival crafting game that replaces the unpaid monster labour with magic and whimsy
A pretty familiar game. Get it? Because of the witches? Ah, nevermind.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Humble's offering a nearly buck-a-game deal on some of the best reactive narratives out there, including one of my personal favourite games of all time
Mon dieu.

author: PC Gamer
text:
There's one AI machine that doesn't need a nuclear power station to run, and it points to a potential way forward in the memory crisis
You, a box, and a crank.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The latest market data shows GPU shipments are holding up for now but AMD isn't making any inroads on Nvidia
Is this the quiet before the storm?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Browser-based dirt bike game is 'Motocross meets Wall Street,' letting you ride Nvidia and Intel's stock charts
Getting revved up.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Extraction shooter peak is finally returning and all you Arc Raiders players should definitely take note
DMZ was doing the whole "accessible PvE-centric extraction shooter" thing long before Arc was.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Dog tries to shoot PC gamer, but their rig (and RAM) takes the bullet instead: 'It would have hit me while I was sleeping if the PC hadn't been there'
Other redditors naturally claim it's just an advanced 'dog ate my homework' excuse.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This DIY motion capture rig features 16 cameras made from scratch and is doing nothing to assuage my Vtubing delusions of grandeur
DI-why not?

author: PC Gamer
text:
This custom fingertip mouse is one of the weirdest I've seen and now you can build one for yourself, though you'll need to sacrifice another mouse first
There is something fitting about this spider-like mouse devouring a different mouse to function.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The BBC quizzed chip maker TSMC on the possibility of increased prices and you're really not going to like the answer
Inflation and TSMC's "value" looks set to push prices ever higher.

author: PC Gamer
text:
WoW Classic players are in an arms race against annoying RMT casinos, but Blizzard's working on strategies so 'they will hopefully piss off'
The house always wins.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Alienware AW2525HM 320 Hz gaming monitor review
An enticing option for competitive FPS gamers on a budget.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Framework has delayed its 'MacBook Pro for Linux users' by a month, citing problems with its display and touchpad
You can still get the Mainboard by June if you've already preordered, though.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Reports suggest Meta was working on another facial recognition feature that was quietly pushed out to millions of users before being just as quietly deleted
Smart glasses continue to be a scourge.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Xbox CCO Matt Booty says he's seen The Elder Scrolls 6, 'it looks amazing, and it's coming along well'
I want to believe.
